description: Lock the 2026-06-27 telescope correction to a 1608 Dutch refracting spyglass/telescope scope with lens-grinding craft as the direct optical workshop dependency.

checks.reason: The node now anchors to the earliest well-documented 1608 Dutch telescope record rather than a generic Renaissance placeholder.
Grinding, polishing, selecting, and mounting lenses is the directly sourced optical craft capability for early refracting telescopes.
The early Dutch and Galilean telescope source evidence supports spectacle and lens-making craft, not a broad formal scientific-instrument guild dependency.
The source traces the early concave-plus-convex lens combination through spectacle makers and their shops.
Earlier knowledge of convex and concave lenses is optical lineage rather than the immediate workshop capability.
